I have the likely hook point: `server.New` can compute the active ddxroot path and emit a log warning if legacy home subdirs still exist. I’m checking the test utilities now so I can assert on the exact warning text without introducing brittle stdout capture.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the tests already have a logger capture helper, so the cleanest path is a `log.Printf` warning in `server.New` plus two focused startup tests. I’m checking the `ddxroot.Path` side effects before I call it from startup so we don’t accidentally create state just by warning.

`ddxroot.Path` bootstraps the convention root, so I’m not going to call it just to format a warning. I’m checking whether the server already has the same root derivation logic I can reuse without creating files as a side effect.

data.output:
package server

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func setupConventionRootWorkerProject(t *testing.T, projectRoot string) *bead.Store {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", t.TempDir())
	t.Setenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR", ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, "exec-worktrees"))

	stateRoot := ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), projectRoot)
	require.NotEqual(t, filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), stateRoot, "test must run in convention-root mode")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(stateRoot, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(stateRoot, "config.yaml"), []byte("version: \"1.0\"\nbead:\n  id_prefix: \"wt\"\n"), 0o644))

	store := bead.NewStore(stateRoot)
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
		ID:         "ddx-root-worker",
		Title:      "Worker DDx root coverage",
		Status:     bead.StatusOpen,
		IssueType:  bead.DefaultType,
		Acceptance: "worker should resolve state via ddxroot",
	}))

	initGitRepo(t, projectRoot)
	return store
}

func TestWorkerManagerUsesDDxRoot(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	store := setupConventionRootWorkerProject(t, projectRoot)

	m := NewWorkerManager(projectRoot)
	m.BeadWorkerFactory = func(s agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore) *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker {
		return &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
			Store: s,
			Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
					BeadID: beadID,
					Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
				}, nil
			}),
		}
	}

	record, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{Mode: "once"})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	final := waitForWorkerExit(t, m, record.ID, 10*time.Second)
	assert.NotEqual(t, "running", final.State)

	statusPath := ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, "workers", record.ID, "status.json")
	data, err := os.ReadFile(statusPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var persisted WorkerRecord
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(data, &persisted))
	assert.Equal(t, projectRoot, persisted.ProjectRoot)

	_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "workers", record.ID))
	assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err), "worker artifacts must not be written under projectRoot/.ddx in convention-root mode")

	events, err := store.Events("ddx-root-worker")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, events, "worker execution should have used the convention-root bead store")
}

data.text: I’ve got the shape now: add a startup-only warning helper, call it from `server.New`, and cover it with two logger-capture tests. I’m editing the server package next, keeping the change isolated to the startup path and test files.
The first patch missed the exact import shape, so I’m re-reading the top of `server.go` and then I’ll patch the startup hook and helper in one pass.

// serverPromptCapBytes caps the size of inline prompt bodies accepted on
// server-side ingress (POST /api/agent/run). FEAT-022 §9 / Stage D2. It is
// a package-level variable rather than a literal so tests can lower the
// cap without writing multi-MB request bodies. Production callers must not
// mutate it.
var serverPromptCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes

// serverBuildSHA returns the build's VCS revision for inclusion on
// operator-prompt audit events. Falls back to "unknown" when build info is
// unavailable (e.g. `go test` without -buildvcs or development builds).

data.text: The code path is in place. I’m adding the two focused startup tests now, each one creating only the legacy home subdir it cares about and capturing the logger output from `server.New`.
I caught one compile risk before running tests: `server.go` picked up `log` but doesn’t use it directly. I’m removing that stray import, then I’ll format and run the server test slice.
The edit is formatted. I’m checking the final shape once, then I’ll run the server package tests to make sure the startup warnings and log capture behave exactly as intended.

The test run is still in flight. I’m polling for the result now; if it fails, I’ll correct the specific compile/test issue rather than broadening scope.

The package test sweep is taking longer than a quick unit run, which usually means the server package is doing real integration work. I’m letting it finish rather than interrupting and risking an incomplete signal.

data.output:
# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    git-config-health:
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."

    beads-tracker-health:
      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
    go-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
        for pkg in $packages; do
          # Unset GIT_DIR so revgrep (used by --new-from-rev) computes the diff
          # against the actual work tree, not an empty one. Git sets GIT_DIR in
          # hook context pointing at the worktree's .git subdir; when a subprocess
          # runs git diff with only GIT_DIR set (no GIT_WORK_TREE), the work tree
          # appears empty and revgrep panics on function-name contexts in hunks.
          env -u GIT_DIR -u GIT_INDEX_FILE golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go build - only for changed packages
    go-build:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Build"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: 'go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...'
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
    evidence-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:

data.text: I’m recording the blocker in the execution bundle now, since the bead can’t be cleanly closed while the required package test gate is already red on unrelated server tests.
I’ve written the blocker note. I’m grabbing the exact line numbers for the code and tests so the handoff points at the right evidence instead of hand-waving at filenames.

data.text:
Implemented the startup warning path, but I couldn’t close the bead because the required package-wide server test gate is already red on unrelated existing tests.

What changed:
- [`cli/internal/server/server.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4ee830ff-20260607T105219-a8c5ae31/cli/internal/server/server.go#L204) now calls `warnAboutLegacyHomeState(workingDir)` during `Server.New`.
- [`cli/internal/server/legacy_home_state_warning.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4ee830ff-20260607T105219-a8c5ae31/cli/internal/server/legacy_home_state_warning.go#L15) adds startup detection for `~/.ddx/server` and `~/.ddx/tsnet`, computes the active project state root, and logs a warning that names both paths.
- [`cli/internal/server/legacy_home_state_warning_test.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4ee830ff-20260607T105219-a8c5ae31/cli/internal/server/legacy_home_state_warning_test.go#L13) adds `TestServerWarnsAboutOrphanHomeServerState` and `TestServerWarnsAboutOrphanHomeTSNetState`.

Validation:
- Passed: `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server -run 'TestServerWarnsAboutOrphanHome(ServerState|TSNetState)$'`
- Failed: `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/...` due existing red tests in the server suite unrelated to this change.
- I recorded that blocker in [`no_changes_rationale.txt`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4ee830ff-20260607T105219-a8c5ae31/.ddx/executions/20260607T105219-a8c5ae31/no_changes_rationale.txt#L1).

If you want, I can next dig into the unrelated server suite failures, but I stopped short of committing because the required package gate is not green.
